title: cerasus avium subsp. juliana
intro: Cerasus avium subsp. juliana, also known as wild cherry or gean, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains a glycoside called amygdalin that has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cerasus avium subsp. juliana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark, such as tinctures and infusions, are often used to treat these conditions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
